---
title: "2.11 Nested Iteration"
subtitle: "Part 1: Rectangle Nested Loops"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### A loop inside another loop

<span class="term">Nested iteration statements</span>, or <span class="term">nested loops</span> (嵌套循环), are iteration statements that appear in the body of another iteration statement.

Or in simpler terms, a loop inside of another loop.

Nested loops are typically used for working with two dimensions, for example printing a rectangle of stars in rows and columns.

## Nested Loop Flow

### Inner loop restarts for each outer iteration

When a loop is nested inside another loop, the inner loop runs many times inside the outer loop.

In each iteration of the outer loop, the inner loop will be re-started.

The inner loop must finish all of its iterations before the outer loop can continue to its next iteration.

## Nested Loop Diagram

### Nested Loops

::: {.image-fit}
![Nested Loops](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-11-nested-iteration/nestedloops.png){fig-align="center" width="48%"}
:::

## Code Task

### `activecode:: lcfcnl1`

Textbook prompt: What does the following code print out?

Notice how the inner loop is started over for each row.

Can you predict how many rows and columns of stars there will be?

## Code Window

### `activecode:: lcfcnl1`

```java
public class NestedLoops
{

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        for (int row = 1; row <= 3; row++)
        {
            for (int col = 1; col <= 5; col++)
            {
                System.out.print("*");
            }
            System.out.println();
        }
    }
}
```

## Code Task

### Change rows and columns

Textbook prompt: Can you change the code to print a rectangle with `10` rows and `8` columns of stars?

You can also try replacing line 10 with this print statement to see the rows and columns:

```java
System.out.print(row + "-" + col + " ");
```

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: lcfcnl1`

Runestone checks that the submitted code contains:

::: {.tight-list}
- `row <= 10`
- `col <= 8`
:::

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: nested1`

What does the following code print?

```java
for (int i = 1; i < 7; i++)
{
    for (int y = 1; y <= 5; y++)
    {
        System.out.print("*");
    }
    System.out.println();
}
```

::: {.tight-list}
- A. A rectangle of 7 rows with 5 stars per row.
- B. A rectangle of 7 rows with 4 stars per row.
- C. A rectangle of 6 rows with 5 stars per row.
- D. A rectangle of 6 rows with 4 stars per row.
:::

## Answer Reasoning

### `mchoice:: nested1`

Correct answer: <span class="mark">C. A rectangle of 6 rows with 5 stars per row.</span>

The outer loop runs from `1` up to `7` but not including `7`, so there are `6` rows.

The inner loop runs `1` to `5` including `5`, so there are `5` columns.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: nested2`

What does the following code print?

```java
for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
{
    for (int j = 3; j >= 1; j--)
    {
        System.out.print("*");
    }
    System.out.println();
}
```

::: {.tight-list}
- A. A rectangle of 4 rows with 3 star per row.
- B. A rectangle of 5 rows with 3 stars per row.
- C. A rectangle of 4 rows with 1 star per row.
- D. The loops have errors.
:::

## Answer Reasoning

### `mchoice:: nested2`

Correct answer: <span class="mark">B. A rectangle of 5 rows with 3 stars per row.</span>

The outer loop runs from `0` up to `5` but not including `5`, so there are `5` rows.

The inner loop runs when `j` is `3`, `2`, and `1`, so there are `3` stars per row.

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: nestedParsons1`

Textbook prompt: The main method in the following class should print `10` rows with `5` `*`s in each row.

The blocks have been mixed up and include one extra block that isn't needed in the solution.

## Mixed Blocks

### `parsonsprob:: nestedParsons1`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class Test1
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
```

```java
        for (int x = 0; x < 10; x++)
        {
```

```java
            for (int y = 0; y < 5; y++)
            {
```

```java
            for (int y = 0; y <= 5; y++)
            { // extra block not needed
```

```java
                System.out.print("*");
```

```java
            }
```

```java
            System.out.println();
```

```java
        }
```

```java
    }
}
```
:::

## Correct Order

### `parsonsprob:: nestedParsons1`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class Test1
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        for (int x = 0; x < 10; x++)
        {
            for (int y = 0; y < 5; y++)
            {
                System.out.print("*");
            }
            System.out.println();
        }
    }
}
```
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- define nested iteration as a loop inside another loop
- explain that the inner loop restarts for each outer-loop iteration
- identify rows as the outer loop and columns as the inner loop in rectangle patterns
- count rows and columns from inclusive and exclusive loop bounds
- reject the extra `y <= 5` block because it would print 6 stars per row
- connect rectangular total work to outer iterations times inner iterations
:::

## End

### Continue to Part 2

[Next: Turtle nested loops and snowflakes →](2-11-part-2-turtle-nested-loops-and-snowflakes.html)
